### TypeScript: Parse Execution Output Line by Line

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.1.1/messaging/page.mdx

The `parseOutput` function processes an `Execution` object and an input `line` asynchronously. It supports optional callback functions for handling stdout, stderr, results, and errors, enabling real-time processing of execution events.

```typescript
function parseOutput(
   execution: Execution, 
   line: string, 
   onStdout?: (output: OutputMessage) => any, 
   onStderr?: (output: OutputMessage) => any, 
   onResult?: (data: Result) => any,
 onError?: (error: ExecutionError) => any): Promise<void>
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Parse Execution Output Line by Line

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.5.0/messaging/page.mdx

Asynchronously parses a single line of output from an execution, handling stdout, stderr, results, and errors. It utilizes optional callback functions for processing each type of output.

```typescript
function parseOutput(
   execution: Execution,
   line: string,
   onStdout?: (output: OutputMessage) => any,
   onStderr?: (output: OutputMessage) => any,
   onResult?: (data: Result) => any,
   onError?: (error: ExecutionError) => any): Promise<void>
```

--------------------------------

### Run Python Code with Callbacks

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v1.0.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Executes Python code within the sandbox and handles output and results via callback functions. This method allows for real-time processing of stdout, stderr, and execution results.

```python
@overload
async def run_code(code: str,
                   language: Union[Literal["python"], None] = None,
                   on_stdout: Optional[OutputHandler[OutputMessage]] = None,
                   on_stderr: Optional[OutputHandler[OutputMessage]] = None,
                   on_result: Optional[OutputHandler[Result]] = None,
                   on_error: Optional[OutputHandler[ExecutionError]] = None,
                   envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                   timeout: Optional[float] = None,
                   request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> Execution
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Parse Execution Output

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.2.0/messaging/page.mdx

The `parseOutput()` function asynchronously processes a line of execution output. It accepts optional callbacks for handling standard output, standard error, results, and execution errors, allowing for granular control over output management.

```typescript
function parseOutput(
   execution: Execution,
   line: string,
   onStdout?: (output: OutputMessage) => any,
   onStderr?: (output: OutputMessage) => any,
   onResult?: (data: Result) => any,
onError?: (error: ExecutionError) => any): Promise<void>
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Parse Execution Output Line by Line

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.0.4/messaging/page.mdx

The `parseOutput()` function asynchronously parses a single line of execution output. It accepts various callback functions to handle standard output (`onStdout`), standard error (`onStderr`), results (`onResult`), and errors (`onError`), allowing for real-time processing of execution feedback.

```typescript
function parseOutput(
   execution: Execution,
   line: string,
   onStdout?: (output: OutputMessage) => any,
   onStderr?: (output: OutputMessage) => any,
   onResult?: (data: Result) => any,
 onError?: (error: ExecutionError) => any): Promise<void>
```

--------------------------------

### Python: Serialize Execution Results

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v2.0.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Serializes a list of Result objects into a list of dictionaries, suitable for JSON output. This function is part of processing and storing execution outcomes.

```python
def serialize_results(results: List[Result]) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
    """Serializes the results to JSON."""
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Python Enum for Chart Types

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v1.1.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Defines an enumeration for different chart types. This is used to categorize and specify the type of charts generated or processed.

```python
class ChartType(str, enum.Enum):
    # Represents chart types
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Get Text Representation of Execution Result

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v1.0.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieves the text representation of an execution result. This is useful for displaying or processing the textual output of a code execution.

```python
# Assuming 'result' is an Execution object from sandbox.run_code()
text_output = result.text
print(f"Text Output: {text_output}")
```

### TypeScript: Implement deserializeChart Function

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.1.0/charts/page.mdx

A function to deserialize chart data from any format into a Chart object. This is crucial for loading and processing chart configurations.

```typescript
function deserializeChart(data: any): Chart;
function deserializeChart(data: any): Chart {
  // Implementation details for deserialization would go here
  return data; // Placeholder return
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define Chart Types Enumeration (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.1.1/charts/page.mdx

Defines an enumeration for different chart types supported by the interpreter. This helps in categorizing and processing various chart representations.

```typescript
enum ChartType {
  BAR = "bar",
  BOX_AND_WHISKER = "box_and_whisker",
  LINE = "line",
  PIE = "pie",
  SCATTER = "scatter",
  SUPERCHART = "superchart",
  UNKNOWN = "unknown"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Run Code (Synchronous Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v1.1.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Executes Python code synchronously. This method is a specialized version of `run_code` for Python, handling stdout, stderr, results, and errors via callbacks.

```APIDOC
## POST /run_code_python

### Description
Executes Python code synchronously. This endpoint is tailored for Python execution and allows for handling standard output, standard error, results, and errors using callback functions.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/run_code_python

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **code** (str) - Required - The Python code snippet to execute.
- **language** (Literal["python"]) - Optional - Specifies the language as Python. Defaults to Python if not provided or if `context` is not specified in the async version.
- **on_stdout** (OutputHandler[OutputMessage]) - Optional - Callback function for stdout messages.
- **on_stderr** (OutputHandler[OutputMessage]) - Optional - Callback function for stderr messages.
- **on_result** (OutputHandler[Result]) - Optional - Callback function for the result object.
- **on_error** (OutputHandler[ExecutionError]) - Optional - Callback function for execution errors.
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Custom environment variables.
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for code execution in seconds.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "code": "import sys\nprint('Python version: ' + sys.version)",
  "on_stdout": "<callback_function>"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **Execution** (object) - An object representing the result of the code execution.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "execution_id": "<execution_id>",
  "status": "completed",
  "result": {
    "stdout": "Python version: 3.9.12\n",
    "stderr": "",
    "value": null
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Define Chart Types Enumeration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.5.1/charts/page.mdx

Defines an enumeration for various chart types supported by the system. This helps in categorizing and processing different chart representations.

```typescript
type ChartType = "bar" |
  "box_and_whisker" |
  "line" |
  "pie" |
  "scatter" |
  "superchart" |
  "unknown";
```

--------------------------------

### Run Code (Python Default)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v2.0.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Executes Python code within the sandbox. Handles standard output, standard error, results, and errors via callbacks.

```APIDOC
## Run Code (Python Default)

### Description
Executes the provided code snippet primarily as Python. It allows for custom handlers for stdout, stderr, execution results, and errors. If no language is specified, Python is assumed.

### Method
`async def run_code(code: str, language: Literal["python"] = None, on_stdout: OutputHandler[OutputMessage] = None, on_stderr: OutputHandler[OutputMessage] = None, on_result: OutputHandler[Result] = None, on_error: OutputHandler[ExecutionError] = None, envs: Dict[str, str] = None, timeout: float = None, request_timeout: float = None) -> Execution`

### Endpoint
N/A (Client-side execution command)

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **code** (str) - Required - The code snippet to execute.
- **language** (Literal["python"] | None) - Optional - Specifies the language as Python. If omitted, Python is used by default.
- **on_stdout** (OutputHandler[OutputMessage] | None) - Optional - A callback function to handle standard output messages.
- **on_stderr** (OutputHandler[OutputMessage] | None) - Optional - A callback function to handle standard error messages.
- **on_result** (OutputHandler[Result] | None) - Optional - A callback function to handle the final execution result.
- **on_error** (OutputHandler[ExecutionError] | None) - Optional - A callback function to handle execution errors.
- **envs** (Dict[str, str] | None) - Optional - A dictionary of custom environment variables to set for the execution.
- **timeout** (float | None) - Optional - The maximum time in seconds allowed for the code execution itself.
- **request_timeout** (float | None) - Optional - The maximum time in seconds allowed for the request to complete.

### Request Example
```python
async def handle_stdout(message):
    print(f"stdout: {message.content}")

async def handle_result(result):
    print(f"Result: {result.value}")

execution = await sandbox.run_code(
    code='print("Hello, World!")\nx = 5 + 3',
    on_stdout=handle_stdout,
    on_result=handle_result
)
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- `Execution` (object) - An object representing the execution details and results.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "exec_abc123",
  "status": "completed",
  "result": {
    "type": "value",
    "value": 8
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Execute Code in E2B Sandbox (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/js/README.md

Demonstrates how to create a sandbox, run a simple code snippet ('x = 1'), and then execute another snippet that modifies and returns a variable ('x+=1; x') within the sandbox. The output is logged to the console. Requires the E2B API key to be set as an environment variable.

```typescript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = await Sandbox.create()
await sbx.runCode('x = 1')

const execution = await sbx.runCode('x+=1; x')
console.log(execution.text)  // outputs 2
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Convert Result to Serializable JSON

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.5.0/messaging/page.mdx

Converts the current result into a JSON-serializable object. This method is crucial for saving, transmitting, or further processing the result data in a standardized format.

```typescript
toJSON(): object
```

--------------------------------

### Create Code Context for Execution

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.2.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new context for running code within the E2B sandbox. Options can specify the working directory, programming language (defaults to Python), and request timeout.

```typescript
createCodeContext(opts?: CreateCodeContextOpts): Promise<Context>
```

--------------------------------

### Run Code API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v1.5.2/sandbox/page.mdx

Executes provided code within a specified or default context. Supports real-time output handling for stdout, stderr, and execution results or errors.

```APIDOC
## POST /run_code

### Description
Runs the code in the specified context. If no context is specified, the default context for the language is used. Previously defined variables, imports, and functions can be referenced.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/run_code

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **code** (str) - Required - Code to execute.
- **context** (Context) - Optional - Concrete context to run the code in. Mutually exclusive with `language`.
- **on_stdout** (OutputHandler[OutputMessage]) - Optional - Callback for stdout messages.
- **on_stderr** (OutputHandler[OutputMessage]) - Optional - Callback for stderr messages.
- **on_result** (OutputHandler[Result]) - Optional - Callback for the `Result` object.
- **on_error** (OutputHandler[ExecutionError]) - Optional - Callback for the `ExecutionError` object.
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Custom environment variables.
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the code execution in seconds.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "code": "print('Hello, world!')",
  "language": "python",
  "timeout": 10
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **Execution** (Execution) - The result object containing execution details.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "exec-123",
  "status": "completed"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Python Dataclass for Sandbox Output Messages

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v1.2.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Represents a single output message from a sandbox code execution. It includes the message content, a timestamp, and a flag indicating if the message is an error. This is crucial for processing and displaying output from executed code.

```python
@dataclass
class OutputMessage():
    line: str
    timestamp: int
    error: bool
```

--------------------------------

### POST /run_code - Run Code (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v1.2.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Executes Python code within the sandbox. Supports standard output, standard error, and result callbacks, along with custom environment variables and timeouts.

```APIDOC
## POST /run_code - Run Code (Python)

### Description
Executes Python code within the sandbox. Supports standard output, standard error, and result callbacks, along with custom environment variables and timeouts. You can reference previously defined variables, imports, and functions in the code.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/run_code`

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **code** (str) - Required - The code to execute.
- **language** (Literal["python"]) - Optional - The language to use for code execution. Defaults to Python if not specified.
- **on_stdout** (OutputHandler[OutputMessage]) - Optional - Callback for stdout messages.
- **on_stderr** (OutputHandler[OutputMessage]) - Optional - Callback for stderr messages.
- **on_result** (OutputHandler[Result]) - Optional - Callback for the `Result` object.
- **on_error** (OutputHandler[ExecutionError]) - Optional - Callback for the `ExecutionError` object.
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Custom environment variables.
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the code execution in seconds.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "code": "print('Hello, world!')",
  "language": "python"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **execution_result** (Execution) - The result of the code execution.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "exit_code": 0,
  "stdout": "Hello, world!\n",
  "stderr": ""
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### AsyncSandbox - run_code (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v1.2.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Executes Python code within the sandbox and handles the execution results.

```APIDOC
## AsyncSandbox.run_code (Python)

### Description
Runs Python code within the sandbox. You can optionally specify callbacks for stdout, stderr, results, and errors. Previously defined variables, imports, and functions can be referenced.

### Method
`sandbox.run_code(code: str, language: Literal["python"], on_stdout: Optional[OutputHandler[OutputMessage]] = None, on_stderr: Optional[OutputHandler[OutputMessage]] = None, on_result: Optional[OutputHandler[Result]] = None, on_error: Optional[OutputHandler[ExecutionError]] = None, envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, timeout: Optional[float] = None, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> Execution`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **code** (str) - The Python code to execute.
- **language** (Literal["python"]) - Specifies that the code should be run using Python.
- **on_stdout** (Optional[OutputHandler[OutputMessage]]) - Callback for stdout messages.
- **on_stderr** (Optional[OutputHandler[OutputMessage]]) - Callback for stderr messages.
- **on_result** (Optional[OutputHandler[Result]]) - Callback for the `Result` object.
- **on_error** (Optional[OutputHandler[ExecutionError]]) - Callback for `ExecutionError` objects.
- **envs** (Optional[Dict[str, str]]) - Custom environment variables.
- **timeout** (Optional[float]) - Timeout for the code execution in seconds.
- **request_timeout** (Optional[float]) - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example
```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import AsyncSandbox

sandbox = await AsyncSandbox.create()

def handle_result(result):
    print(f"Result: {result.text}")

execution = await sandbox.run_code(
    code="print('Hello, world!')\nresult = 1 + 2",
    language="python",
    on_result=handle_result
)

print(f"Execution count: {execution.execution_count}")
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **execution** (Execution) - An `Execution` object containing the results, logs, and execution count.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "execution_count": 1,
  "results": [
    {
      "type": "text",
      "value": "Hello, world!"
    }
  ],
  "logs": {
    "stdout": [],
    "stderr": []
  },
  "error": null
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Define Chart Types Enum

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.2.0/charts/page.mdx

Defines the enumeration for different chart types. This is used to specify the kind of chart being created or processed. Possible values include BAR, BOX_AND_WHISKER, LINE, PIE, SCATTER, SUPERCHART, and UNKNOWN.

```typescript
type ChartType = 
  | "bar"
  | "box_and_whisker"
  | "line"
  | "pie"
  | "scatter"
  | "superchart"
  | "unknown";
```

--------------------------------

### run_code (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v1.0.4/sandbox/page.mdx

Executes the provided code using the Python interpreter within the sandbox. This method allows for specifying output handlers and environment variables.

```APIDOC
## POST /run_code (Python)

### Description
Runs the provided code as Python. You can specify callbacks for stdout, stderr, results, and errors, as well as custom environment variables and timeouts.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/run_code

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **language** (string) - Optional - Language to use for code execution. Defaults to Python.

#### Request Body
- **code** (string) - Required - The code to execute.
- **on_stdout** (OutputHandler[OutputMessage]) - Optional - Callback for stdout messages.
- **on_stderr** (OutputHandler[OutputMessage]) - Optional - Callback for stderr messages.
- **on_result** (OutputHandler[Result]) - Optional - Callback for the `Result` object.
- **on_error** (OutputHandler[ExecutionError]) - Optional - Callback for the `ExecutionError` object.
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Custom environment variables.
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the code execution in seconds.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "code": "print('Hello, world!')",
  "language": "python"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **Execution** (object) - The result object containing execution details.
```

--------------------------------

### Utility Functions

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/code-interpreter/blob/main/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.0.4/messaging/page.mdx

Documentation for utility functions like `extractError` and `parseOutput` used for handling API responses and execution results.

```APIDOC
## Utility Functions

### Description
Functions that assist in processing API responses and execution outputs.

### Functions

#### `extractError()`

```ts
function extractError(res: Response): Promise<undefined | SandboxError>
```

Extracts error information from a `Response` object.

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `res` | `Response` |

#### Returns

`Promise`<`undefined` | `SandboxError`> - A promise that resolves to either a `SandboxError` object or `undefined` if no error is found.

***

#### `parseOutput()`

```ts
function parseOutput(
   execution: Execution,
   line: string,
   onStdout?: (output: OutputMessage) => any,
   onStderr?: (output: OutputMessage) => any,
   onResult?: (data: Result) => any,
   onError?: (error: ExecutionError) => any): Promise<void>
```

Parses the output of an execution, handling standard output, standard error, results, and errors.

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type |
| ------ | ------ |
| `execution` | `Execution` | The execution context. |
| `line` | `string` | The current line of output to parse. |
| `onStdout`? | (`output`: `OutputMessage`) => `any` | Optional callback for standard output. |
| `onStderr`? | (`output`: `OutputMessage`) => `any` | Optional callback for standard error. |
| `onResult`? | (`data`: `Result`) => `any` | Optional callback for successful results. |
| `onError`? | (`error`: `ExecutionError`) => `any` | Optional callback for execution errors. |

#### Returns

`Promise`<`void`> - A promise that resolves when parsing is complete.
```
